Identified root cause: sessions store base agent pubkey instead of ordinal pubkey, causing both ordinals to match mentions to base key; fix writes ordinal pubkey at registration.

this doesn't make sense -- why would they clobber each other? the pubkey of the first instance of the agent is completely different than the one of the second instance -- this fix doesn't make sense to me -- explain further

the message is directed to a specific pubkey! the fact that its an instance of the base agent should be completely irrelevant! we need to route via pubkey+h-tag -- explain with more detail because something is not adding up and your fix direction is just making the problem bigger

Clarified routing already uses pubkey+h-tag; the bug is `sessions.agent_pubkey` holds base key not ordinal key, causing both sessions to match base mentions; fix makes data honest by writing ordinal key at registration.
born with the right pubkey, obviously!
Refactored to determine session identity before row creation so session is born with ordinal pubkey from start, eliminating after-the-fact preservation; 277 lib tests pass.
